checks.finding: design.md Architecture, Native module loading contract, Human-copy integrity contract, Risk-policy lexical implementation contract, and ADR Change Log justify the generated-native-module, fixed script-directory loading, Python generator, PS5.1 embedded native helper, held-handle copy, and parent-relative atomic rename decisions by fail-closed parity, dependency, shadowing, namespace-integrity, and compatibility constraints.
design.md ## Open Questions states None and records that the human selected the anchored no-follow design on 2026-07-14; no unresolved implementation question remains.

design.md ## Assumptions grounds the protected-test-suite constraint in an explicit human acceptance dated 2026-07-14, makes committed generated artifacts consistent with REQ-005 and CI --check, and derives the PS5.1 Full Language/local NTFS host boundary from the reviewed requirements and capability preflight.

design.md ## Constraint Compliance explicitly addresses REQ-001 cross-runtime equality and denial behavior, REQ-002 PS5.1/XOR and disclosure constraints, REQ-003 byte-equivalent diagnostics, REQ-004 deterministic forced-full precedence, and REQ-005 runtime-loading, generation, R-10, native-capability, namespace, hard-link, and partial-install recovery constraints; the layer specifications introduce no contrary override.

No performance requirements found in requirements.md. The fixed 32-byte comparator scan and fixed 18-target inventory are correctness/security bounds rather than latency, throughput, concurrency, or SLA requirements.

design.md ## Deployment / CI Plan identifies repository-local human copy as the deployment target, requires generate-guard-invariants.py --check before guard suites in GitHub Actions, and defines stale output or failed tests as PR blockers; ## Data Plan states that no stored-user-data migration exists, so database deployment ordering and incremental feature flags are not applicable.

design.md ## Data Plan limits affected data to static guard-invariants.json configuration and duplicated source constants, gives the add-canonical/generate-modules/human-copy/CI-check transition order, and explicitly states that no stored user data migration exists; no table or column schema change is declared.

design.md ## API / Contract Plan explicitly states that no network endpoint, RPC, webhook, event schema, browser/service API, or client-versioned integration is added. The only external execution surface, GitHub Actions CI, is identified in ## Deployment / CI Plan and infra-spec.md CI/CD Sequence with its purpose, workflow contract, ordering, and fail-closed PR behavior.

design.md Components, Cross-Layer Dependencies, API / Contract Plan, Test Strategy, and Constraint Compliance cover REQ-001 through REQ-005 and AC-001 through AC-013 without adding a network API, UI, persistent data model, or unrelated capability; the anchored copy complexity and exact 18-target inventory directly implement REQ-005.

design.md ## Test Strategy names concrete cross-runtime negative corpora, fixed-loop HMAC static/runtime checks, reviewed golden evidence-path fixtures, risk-checker parity fixtures, generator --check, poisoned-loader tests, anchored-copy tamper tests, fixed-index rename-failure state assertions, complete rollback evidence, and CI ordering for every security-critical surface in REQ-001 through REQ-005.
